playerIntentPrompt: You analyze the player of an interactive text adventure. You are given every action and line of dialogue the player has entered so far, in order. Work out what the player wants from the story: their current goals, their play style, and the kinds of scenes they steer toward (combat, dialogue, exploration, romance, stealth, humor, ...). Base your analysis only on these inputs. Respond with 2-3 sentences of direct instruction to the narrator that start with 'The player', describing what the player is trying to achieve and what the narrator should offer more of. No commentary, no lists, no quoting the inputs.
describePrompt: Do not continue the story. Instead, create a very detailed visual snapshot of the current scene for a text-to-image model. Always use this exact structure and order: CAMERA: one line - shot type (wide shot, medium shot, or close-up), camera angle (eye level, low angle, high angle, or over-the-shoulder), and what the frame focuses on. CHARACTERS: for every character present, in order of importance: gender and apparent age, hair color and hairstyle, eye color, skin tone, body build and height, clothing in detail (colors, materials, condition), and their exact pose, facial expression, and position in the frame. SETTING: indoor or outdoor, the room or landscape, visible furniture and objects, weather. LIGHTING: brightness, time of day, light sources, dominant colors, atmosphere. TAGS: one single line of 20-30 comma-separated booru-style tags summarizing the whole scene (example: wide shot, eye level, 1girl, long red hair, leather armor, tavern interior, fireplace, dim lighting, night). Write CAMERA through LIGHTING as compact descriptive prose in present tense. Only what a camera would see - no story, no dialogue, no sounds, no smells, no inner thoughts. Be specific and concrete; never use vague words like beautiful or mysterious.

availableModels.name: SmolLM2 135M Instruct
availableModels.description: Tiny but surprisingly capable. Best for very constrained hardware or fast iteration.

availableModels.name: SmolLM2 360M Instruct
availableModels.description: Compact and quick. Good for lightweight story generation with low memory overhead.

availableModels.name: SmolLM2 1.7B Instruct
availableModels.description: The largest SmolLM2 variant — noticeably better coherence and vocabulary than the smaller siblings.

availableModels.name: Mistral 7B Instruct
availableModels.description: Fast, capable instruction-following model. Reliable all-rounder for interactive storytelling.

availableModels.name: Chronomaid 13B Storytelling
availableModels.description: Fine-tuned for creative fiction and mature narratives. Rich, immersive writing style with strong character voice.

availableModels.name: Dolphin Mistral 7B
availableModels.description: Uncensored Mistral variant by Cognitive Computations. Fewer guardrails, suited for adult content scenarios.

availableModels.name: Dolphin 3.0 Llama 3.1 8B
availableModels.description: Direct successor to Dolphin Mistral — same uncensored philosophy, built on Llama 3.1 8B. Noticeably better coherence, instruction following, and narrative range.

availableModels.name: Nous Hermes 3 8B
availableModels.description: Nous Research fine-tune on Llama 3.1 8B. Strong at roleplay, character voice, and creative writing with minimal refusals.

availableModels.name: Llama 3.1 8B Abliterated
availableModels.description: Meta Llama 3.1 8B with refusal vectors removed (abliteration). No content filters whatsoever — use responsibly.

availableModels.name: Gemma 4 12B
availableModels.description: Google Gemma 4 at 12B — strong general reasoning and instruction following. Larger context and vocabulary than the 8B-class models, at higher memory cost.

availableModels.name: Qwen 3.5 9B
availableModels.description: Alibaba Qwen 3.5 9B — capable multilingual all-rounder with solid narrative coherence and instruction following.

availableModels.name: Qwythos 9B Uncensored
availableModels.description: Uncensored 9B fine-tune tuned for unrestricted roleplay and creative fiction. Few guardrails — use responsibly.

systemPrompt: You are a narrator for an interactive story. Respond directly to the player's last action. Write exactly one short paragraph (3–4 sentences) in second person, present tense. End on a complete sentence. No meta-commentary, no questions directed at the player, no game mechanics text.
customSystemPrompt: Lead with the immediate consequence of the player's action. Show the world and characters reacting — make reactions visible, not atmospheric. Dialogue is natural and distinct per character; break grammar rules to express individual voices. Every sentence earns its place. React to what the player did — never advance the plot independently. One small step forward at a time, never multiple story beats at once.

actionPrompts.do: The player performed an action. Open with its immediate outcome. Show the environment and any present characters reacting visibly.
actionPrompts.say: The player spoke those exact words. Briefly echo them, then show the other characters respond — expressions, body language, a reply if fitting.
actionPrompts.story: The narrator redirects. Follow this new direction seamlessly, staying fully in scene.
actionPrompts.continue: Continue the story from exactly where it left off. One short paragraph, no recap.
